stimulus: Hey — I'm really sorry, work exploded and I can't make dinner tonight. Can we do next week instead? I'll book somewhere good.

interpretation.what_changed: You cancelled again for work, third time in a row.
interpretation.why_it_matters: It confirms my fear that work will always win, and our standing tradition is just a nice idea to you.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: Next week is a chance to see if you really mean it or it's another empty promise.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If I push too hard now, you might resent me; if I say nothing, I enable the neglect.

samples.internal_reaction: A little more resigned now, less hopeful.
samples.decision_summary: I'll text back now, acknowledging the cancellation but gently pointing out the pattern, then accepting next week's offer.

interpretation.what_changed: You cancelled again, third time in two months for work.
interpretation.why_it_matters: Pattern is starting to feel less like bad luck and more like your default priority.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: Next week could be a fresh start; I can note if you actually follow through.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If next week also falls through, I'll need to reconsider how much I stretch.

samples.internal_reaction: Slightly more tired of being the flexible one, but still calm.
samples.decision_summary: Quickly agree to next week, signal understanding so you don't feel worse.

interpretation.what_changed: You cancelled again for work, the third time this month, confirming my suspicion that I'm an afterthought.
interpretation.why_it_matters: This isn't a one-off; it's a clear signal that your priorities don't include me, and I need to stop pretending otherwise.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: I can use this moment to have an honest conversation about where we stand, rather than silently pulling away.
interpretation.perceived_threats: You might dismiss my feelings or make another empty promise, confirming the friendship is over.

samples.internal_reaction: Hurt and more convinced than before that this friendship is one-sided.
samples.decision_summary: I'll reply now, expressing that I understand work happens but this pattern stings, and ask directly if this friendship is still a priority for them.

interpretation.what_changed: You cancelled again for work, third time in two months — the pattern is solidifying, not an isolated incident.
interpretation.why_it_matters: It confirms my belief that work always wins, and that I'm the only one fighting to keep this tradition alive.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: This is a chance to see if you'll actually follow through on 'next week' and book something good, proving the friendship still matters to you.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If you cancel next week too, I'll have to accept the friendship is dying by neglect, and I might need to pull back emotionally.

samples.internal_reaction: Hurt and a little tired, but still holding onto a thread of hope that this time will be different.
samples.decision_summary: I'll reply now, keeping it honest but measured — acknowledging the pattern without full confrontation, to see if you notice and care.

interpretation.what_changed: You cancelled again for work, repeating the pattern from last month.
interpretation.why_it_matters: It tests my patience and raises the question of whether this is a new normal, but I still believe your apology is sincere.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: We can still save the dinner by rescheduling, and I can set a gentle boundary if it happens again.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If this becomes routine, I may start feeling taken for granted, which could erode the friendship.

samples.internal_reaction: Calm but slightly tired of being the flexible one.
samples.decision_summary: I'll reply now, accepting the cancellation and the offer to book next week, keeping the tone warm but brief to avoid lingering on the disappointment.

interpretation.what_changed: They cancelled again at the last minute, repeating the exact pattern I was testing.
interpretation.why_it_matters: This is the third cancellation in two months, all for work, and they offered to 'book somewhere good' as if that fixes the effort imbalance.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: This is a clear signal—either I name the pattern now, or I accept that this friendship is on their terms only.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If I say nothing, I'll resent myself for not drawing a line. If I say too much, I might lose the friendship entirely.

samples.internal_reaction: Hurt and tired, not angry yet, but the disappointment is solidifying into resolve.
samples.decision_summary: I will not reply immediately. I need a few hours to decide if I even want to reschedule, or if this is the final sign to let go.

interpretation.what_changed: Another last-minute cancellation for work, confirming the pattern I feared.
interpretation.why_it_matters: It shows work consistently wins over our friendship, and my elaborate dinner effort didn't change that.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: This is a chance to honestly express my hurt before resentment builds too much.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If I push too hard, you might pull away entirely; if I say nothing, the friendship dies slowly.

samples.internal_reaction: Feeling more hurt and less hopeful than before, but still willing to give one more chance.
samples.decision_summary: Reply now with a gentle but honest statement about feeling hurt, to test if you'll truly follow through on 'next week'.

interpretation.what_changed: You cancelled again, third time in two months, all for work.
interpretation.why_it_matters: It's a pattern now, not a one-off, and I need to decide if I'm okay with that long-term.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: We can still salvage the dinner next week if you follow through.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If this keeps happening, I might start feeling resentful or devalued.

samples.internal_reaction: Slightly more tired and a bit more guarded than before.
samples.decision_summary: I'll text back quickly, acknowledge the situation, and agree to reschedule, but add a small lighthearted remark about hoping next week actually works.

interpretation.what_changed: He cancelled again, same excuse, same last-minute timing. This is the third cancellation in a row.
interpretation.why_it_matters: It confirms my suspicion that work is always his priority over me. The pattern isn't changing.
interpretation.perceived_opportunities: This gives me a chance to see if he notices my silence or reaches out unprompted.
interpretation.perceived_threats: If I say nothing, he might assume everything is fine and keep doing this.

samples.internal_reaction: More resolved than before; the disappointment feels like clarity now.
samples.decision_summary: I need to sit with this before responding. A quick 'sure, next week' would let him off the hook too easily. I'll reply tomorrow morning, calmly and honestly.
